leaderRationale: Accepted delta work improved the authority model, arbitration mechanics, and fail-closed terminal-state mapping, but the package is still not freezable. `cycle-postmortem.json` remains semantically mis-modeled against the source authority: the governing docs define it as job-specific and triggered when the job reaches `frozen`, `blocked`, or `resume_required`, while the artifact still places it under a run-scoped terminal evidence section and requires it for `pushback` completion. Because the self-check and fix-acceptance narrative marked that separation as resolved, the evidence package still overclaims compliance and is not yet trustworthy enough for freeze.
unresolved: group-1: Cycle post-mortem scope and terminal closeout rules are still semantically inconsistent with the source authority.
group-2: The self-check and fix-acceptance narrative overclaim closure, so the review evidence is not fully trustworthy yet.

group-2-r0: Separated job-scoped from run-scoped evidence for cycle-postmortem throughout the artifact. (1) <outputs> cycle-postmortem row now carries a 'Job-scoped.' label with lifecycle: 'Write once when the job (all attempts) reaches its terminal state. If the job is resumed and reaches a new terminal state, replace with the final job-spanning summary. Per-attempt history is preserved in run-postmortem.json.' (2) run-postmortem row now annotates 'Scope: single-attempt KPI snapshot.' (3) Step 5 promotion rules add an explicit paragraph defining when cycle-postmortem.json is written at job-level terminal closeout and what happens on resume. Prior lifecycle wording 'when the run reaches terminal closeout' has been removed.
improvementsApplied: Separated operative authority from reference evidence and replaced hardcoded run-specific reference paths with `<job-id>` templates.
Replaced the locally invented arbitration threshold with a carried-forward conceptual-group trigger and kept arbitration limited to `minor` and `suggestion` disputes.
Added explicit fail-closed terminal-state mapping for caller-resolvable gaps, unrecoverable errors, and budget exhaustion.
Clarified the intended job-level nature of `cycle-postmortem.json` and its relationship to per-attempt `run-postmortem.json`, although the lifecycle is still not fully reconciled.

conceptual_groups.summary: Cycle post-mortem scope and terminal closeout rules are still semantically inconsistent with the source authority.

findings.description: The artifact still gives cycle-postmortem.json two competing scope definitions: it sits inside the section titled 'Run-scoped terminal evidence' while its own row labels it 'Job-scoped.' That leaves the lifecycle model non-canonical for a single artifact.

The pushback completion rule still requires cycle-postmortem.json, but the source authority defines the Tier 2 cycle post-mortem trigger only for frozen, blocked, and resume_required terminal outcomes. This widens terminal behavior beyond the cited authority.

conceptual_groups.redesign_guidance: Give cycle-postmortem.json one canonical classification as job-scoped evidence, remove it from run-scoped headings, and align every trigger/completion reference to the source-authority terminal set unless an operative contract explicitly broadens that trigger.

conceptual_groups.summary: The self-check and fix-acceptance narrative overclaim closure, so the review evidence is not fully trustworthy yet.

findings.description: The review request says 'Self-check: passed' and '24 rules checked' but does not show any explicit ARB-012 evaluation, even though reviewer-pair validation is a required focus area for this review.
findings.source_section: Review request / Self-check and compliance map summary

findings.description: The accepted fix note for group-2-r0 says job-scoped and run-scoped evidence were separated 'throughout the artifact,' but the delivered artifact still keeps cycle-postmortem.json inside a run-scoped section. The acceptance note therefore overstates what was actually fixed.
findings.source_section: Review request / Fix items map from implementer: group-2-r0; <outputs> / Run-scoped terminal evidence

conceptual_groups.redesign_guidance: Do not mark the round as fully passed until every required ARB rule has an explicit check result, and keep fix-acceptance notes exact to the delivered text. If a remediation is partial, record it as partial instead of complete.

fix_decisions.reason: The authority model issue was resolved: reference docs are now explicitly non-operative and the run-specific hardcoded path was replaced with the <job-id> template.
The lifecycle wording improved, but the artifact still places cycle-postmortem.json under a run-scoped section and still requires it for pushback completion, so the separation is incomplete.
Arbitration is now keyed to carried-forward conceptual-group IDs and the fail-closed terminal-state mapping is explicit, removing the earlier locally invented threshold ambiguity.

residual_risks: Reviewer-roster legality is intentionally inherited rather than restated, so final freeze still depends on the active review contract explicitly enforcing the Codex-plus-Claude pair gate during request validation.
Budget-exhaustion ultimatum behavior is mostly inherited by reference, so later revisions should keep verifying that the operative contracts still define the one-fix-per-reviewer closeout path.
strengths: Operative authority and reference evidence are now cleanly separated.
Material pushback has one canonical definition and is reused consistently.
Context-gathering is correctly framed as a precondition rather than a numbered execution step.
Arbitration is mechanically limited to minor or suggestion disagreements and tied to carried-forward conceptual-group IDs.
Decision-history preservation is explicit and append-only for update and fix work.
